brief.name: Lindy
brief.what_they_do: Lindy is an AI executive assistant that handles administrative work like email triage, meeting scheduling, and follow-ups via iMessage, SMS, Gmail, and Outlook. It learns user communication style and integrates with hundreds of apps to automate inbox management, calendar coordination, and task tracking.
key_features: Email triage and drafting in user's voice

signals.detail: iMessage is prominently featured in the hero section with video demo showing 'Lindy›iMessage' conversation thread; pricing tier explicitly lists 'Chat via iMessage / SMS' as a feature
SMS communication highlighted in pricing: 'Chat via iMessage / SMS'; email triage section states 'I'll text you about important emails so you never miss what matters'
Product supports Gmail, Outlook, Slack, iMessage, and SMS across multiple integrations; 'hundreds of integrations' mentioned
Customer logos include Shopify, Apple, Adobe, McKinsey, NVIDIA, Harvard, Airbnb, Stanford, Autodesk, AppLovin
Plus tier at $49.99/month, Pro tier at $59.99/month, Enterprise with custom pricing; free 7-day trial offered
GDPR, HIPAA, AICPA, and PIPEDA compliant; privacy-first design with encryption standard; data never sold or used to train models
The AI Price War Is Here, Piling Pressure on OpenAI and Anthropic — Startups and tech giants alike are mixing and matching AI models to avoid the premium prices charged by industry leaders.
Lindy’s Diner owners wrestle with how to handle next hurdle of demolition process — The latest videos from KRQE NEWS 13 - Breaking News, Albuquerque News, New Mexico News, Weather, and Videos.

AI startups are leasing luxury apartments in San Francisco for staff and offering large rent stipends to attract talent — The average rent increase in San Francisco leads all other cities in the past year, according to real estate data.
Hacker News title mentions of "Lindy": 102 (live count, hackernews.hackernews) — real public-builder attention exists.

Photon — open-source iMessage infrastructure for AI agents. imessage-kit: ~2,300 GitHub stars, 20K+ developers. Ditto: 42,000+ users across 5 campuses, 99.87% delivery. We sell the channel agents' users actually open.
PERSON SCRAPE DEGRADED: person stage skipped: no person inputs on the run (linkedinUrl/xHandle/name all absent)
imessage-kit: ~2,300 GitHub stars, 20K+ developers, github.com/photon-hq/imessage-kit

Ditto: 42,000+ users across 5 campuses (UCSD, Berkeley, USC, UCLA, UC Davis), 20% of matches convert to in-person dates, 99.87% message delivery, zero downtime during Apple throttling events, $9.2M seed from Peak XV Partners
TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on the SDK ($47M Series A, ICONIQ Growth)
MiroFish simulation: 'infrastructure durability' (42 likes) vs '98% open rate' (2 likes) — resilience resonates 21x
We shipped a full SDK in one week, infrastructure in one month

brief.brief: Lindy is an AI executive assistant that automates administrative work (email, meetings, follow-ups) and communicates via iMessage, SMS, Gmail, and Slack. The product is already live with iMessage as a core messaging channel and has achieved significant enterprise adoption (Apple, Shopify, McKinsey, etc.). With strong messaging channel integration and agentic notification capabilities, Lindy represents a proven use case for intelligent multi-channel communication infrastructure.

event.story:
THE GOAL:
Lindy wants to be the AI executive assistant that actually replaces a human EA — not just scheduling meetings but proactively surfacing what matters, drafting in your voice, and reaching you wherever you already live. Their site hero shows a live 'Lindy›iMessage' thread. The bet is clear: the agent is only as good as the channel it reaches you on [imessage_featured].

THE OBSTACLE:
Lindy's iMessage integration is load-bearing — it's how the agent texts you about important emails, sends reminders, and closes the loop on action items [sms_featured]. That means when Apple throttles iMessage delivery (which it does, without warning), Lindy's core value prop — 'I'll text you so you never miss what matters' — breaks silently. The user just... misses what matters. With enterprise logos like Apple, McKinsey, and NVIDIA on the page [enterprise_adoption], a delivery failure isn't a minor bug. It's a trust event.

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
Building iMessage delivery in-house means owning the throttling problem yourself. There's no public API — every team that's done this has had to reverse-engineer stability, handle rate limits, and eat the downtime. The multi-channel surface Lindy already supports (Gmail, Outlook, Slack, iMessage, SMS [multi_channel_messaging]) means engineering attention is spread thin. A home-built iMessage layer doesn't get better over time; it gets more brittle as Apple tightens the screws and the team is focused on the AI layer, not the pipe.

THE BETTER PATH:
Photon's imessage-kit is open-source iMessage infrastructure built specifically for agents — ~2,300 GitHub stars, 20K+ developers [seller_proof]. Ditto ran 42,000+ users across 5 campuses with 99.87% message delivery and zero downtime during Apple throttling events [seller_proof]. TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on the SDK after a $47M Series A [seller_proof]. The Advanced Kit adds tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts, voice messages, and edit/recall — the signals that make an AI feel like a real conversation partner, not a notification bot [seller_pack]. Spectrum (photon.codes/spectrum) layers iMessage, Telegram, and Discord under one SDK if Lindy ever wants to expand the channel surface without re-architecting [seller_pack].

The one-line angle: your agent is only as reliable as the pipe it rides — we've already solved the throttling problem so you don't have to.

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lindy's iMessage delivery holds at enterprise scale without a dedicated infrastructure engineer watching it. The agent earns the trust it promises — 'you'll never miss what matters' becomes a guarantee, not a hope. Advanced features like typing indicators and tapbacks make Lindy feel less like a bot and more like the EA it's positioning itself as. And if enterprise buyers ever push for audit-ready delivery logs, the infrastructure is already built for that.
